name: Roof Replacement
short_description: Full tear-off and new roof installation backed by manufacturer warranties.
meta_description: Full roof replacement from Linta Roofing — complete tear-off, new decking and underlayment, and quality shingle installation backed by manufacturer warranties. Built to handle Carolina coastal weather.
hero_heading:
Roof Replacement

When repairs no longer make sense, a full roof replacement protects your home and your investment for decades. Linta Roofing handles every step — from tear-off to final cleanup — using materials engineered for coastal weather and backed by manufacturer warranties.

Get Free EstimateContact Us
signs_body:
Signs You Need a Roof Replacement

  • Your roof is over 20 years old
  • Shingles are curling, cracking, or missing in multiple areas
  • Granules from shingles are collecting in your gutters
  • You see daylight through your attic boards
  • Your roof deck is sagging or shows water damage
  • Repairs are becoming frequent and expensive
cost_body:
Roof Replacement Cost

Most full roof replacements in our service area fall between $8,000 and $25,000, depending on roof size, pitch, materials, and complexity. We provide free, no-pressure inspections with a detailed written estimate so you know exactly what your project will cost — no surprises, no upsells.
benefits_body:
Benefits of a Roof Replacement

  • Decades of protection backed by manufacturer warranties
  • Improved energy efficiency and lower utility bills
  • Higher home resale value and curb appeal
  • Reduced risk of leaks, mold, and structural damage
  • Peace of mind through hurricane season
faq_body:
How long does a roof replacement take?

Most residential roof replacements are completed in one to three days, weather permitting.

Will my home be exposed during the replacement?

No. We tear off and replace your roof in the same day so your home is never left vulnerable overnight.

Does insurance cover roof replacement?

If your roof was damaged by a covered event such as a storm, your homeowner's insurance often covers replacement. We assist with documentation and the claim process.

name: Roof Repair
short_description: Targeted repairs for leaks, missing shingles, and storm damage.
meta_description: Fast, reliable roof repair in coastal Carolina. Linta Roofing fixes leaks, missing shingles, flashing failures, and storm damage — same-week service and a workmanship warranty on every repair.
hero_heading:
Roof Repair

A small roof problem can quickly become a major one. Linta Roofing's repair team identifies the root cause — not just the symptom — and delivers fixes that hold up against wind, rain, and salt air. Same-week service for active leaks.

Get Free InspectionContact Us
signs_body:
Signs You Need a Roof Repair

  • Active leaks or water stains on ceilings or walls
  • Missing, cracked, or lifted shingles
  • Damaged or rusted flashing around chimneys and vents
  • Granules from shingles in your gutters or downspouts
  • Visible sagging or soft spots on the roof deck
  • Recent storm or wind event in your area
cost_body:
Roof Repair Cost

Most roof repairs in our service area fall between $350 and $1,500, depending on the type and extent of the damage. Larger repairs involving multiple slopes, chimney rebuilds, or significant decking replacement may run higher. Free inspections, free estimates, no pressure.
benefits_body:
Benefits of Timely Roof Repairs

  • Stops leaks before they become major water damage
  • Extends the lifespan of your existing roof
  • Protects your insulation and attic structure
  • Maintains your home's resale value
  • Often eligible for insurance coverage on storm-related damage
faq_body:
How quickly can you respond to a leak?

For active leaks, we offer same-week service and emergency tarping when needed.

Do you guarantee your repair work?

Yes. Every Linta Roofing repair is backed by a written workmanship warranty.

When is repair better than replacement?

If your roof is under 15 years old and the damage is localized, repair is usually the right choice. We'll give you a straight answer during the free inspection.

name: Roof Inspection
short_description: Free 21-point inspection with photo report and repair recommendations.
meta_description: Free 21-point roof inspection from Linta Roofing. Photo-documented report, honest recommendations, and no high-pressure upsell. Schedule yours today.
hero_heading:
Roof Inspection

A thorough inspection is the foundation of every smart roofing decision. Linta Roofing's free 21-point inspection includes a photo-documented report, an honest assessment, and clear recommendations — repair, maintenance, or replacement — with no pressure to buy.

Book Free InspectionContact Us
signs_body:
When to Get a Roof Inspection

  • After a major wind, hail, or hurricane event
  • When buying or selling a home
  • Annually, especially for roofs over 10 years old
  • When you notice ceiling stains, attic moisture, or higher energy bills
  • Before filing an insurance claim
  • If a tree, debris, or animal has been on your roof
cost_body:
Roof Inspection Cost

Linta Roofing's standard inspection is free for homeowners in our service area. Detailed forensic reports for real-estate transactions or insurance disputes are available for a flat fee — we'll explain all options up front.
benefits_body:
Benefits of a Professional Inspection

  • Catches small issues before they become expensive repairs
  • Documents existing conditions for insurance and resale
  • Verifies remaining lifespan of your current roof
  • Identifies ventilation, flashing, and decking issues invisible from the ground
  • Provides a written report you can keep on file
faq_body:
How long does an inspection take?

Most residential inspections are completed in 60 to 90 minutes, including the report walkthrough.

Do I need to be home?

You don't have to be — but we recommend it so we can review the findings with you in person.

Will you try to sell me a new roof?

No. If your roof has years left, we'll tell you so. Honest assessments are the foundation of our reputation.

name: Storm Damage
short_description: Wind, hail, and hurricane damage assessment with insurance-claim support.
meta_description: Fast storm damage response from Linta Roofing — wind, hail, and hurricane damage assessments, emergency tarping, and full restoration. Insurance claim assistance from start to finish.
hero_heading:
Storm Damage

When a storm hits the Carolina coast, fast action protects your home and your insurance claim. Linta Roofing provides emergency tarping, complete damage assessments, and full restoration — and we handle the insurance paperwork from start to finish.

Schedule Damage AssessmentContact Us
signs_body:
Signs of Storm Damage

  • Missing, cracked, or displaced shingles after high winds
  • Dents or bruising on shingles, vents, or gutters from hail
  • Tree limbs, debris, or punctures on the roof surface
  • Water stains on ceilings or walls following a storm
  • Granules washed into gutters or downspouts
  • Damaged flashing, ridge caps, or soffit
cost_body:
Storm Damage Restoration Cost

If the damage is covered by your homeowner's insurance, your out-of-pocket cost is typically just your deductible. Linta Roofing meets your adjuster on-site, documents every covered item, and ensures the scope of work matches the damage. We don't get paid until the work is done right.
benefits_body:
Working With Linta on Storm Restoration

  • Experienced with insurance claims and adjuster meetings
  • Emergency tarping to prevent further damage
  • Full documentation with photos and reports
  • Direct billing to insurance when permitted
  • Local team — not a storm-chaser from out of state
faq_body:
Should I file a claim before or after I call you?

Call us first. We'll inspect your roof for free and tell you whether the damage rises to the level of a claim. Filing a claim that gets denied can affect your insurance.

How long do I have to file a storm claim?

Most insurance carriers require claims within one year of the storm event, but the sooner the better — evidence fades over time.

Will my insurance go up?

Storm-related claims are typically not the same as at-fault claims and rarely cause individual rate increases. We can talk through the specifics during your inspection.

name: Metal Roofing
short_description: Standing seam, metal shingle, and specialty metal systems engineered for coastal durability.
meta_description: Metal roofing from Linta Roofing — standing seam, metal shingles, and specialty systems with 40+ year lifespans. Built to handle hurricane winds and salt-air corrosion.
hero_heading:
Metal Roofing

Metal roofs deliver decades of protection with minimal maintenance — the right choice for coastal homes that face high winds, salt air, and intense sun. Linta Roofing installs standing seam, metal shingle, and specialty systems backed by 40-year manufacturer warranties.

Get Free EstimateContact Us
signs_body:
Reasons to Choose Metal Roofing

  • Looking for a roof that will outlast your mortgage
  • Living in a high-wind or hurricane-prone coastal zone
  • Want to reduce attic temperatures and energy bills
  • Need a Class A fire-rated roof system
  • Concerned about salt-air corrosion damaging asphalt shingles
  • Want a long-term, low-maintenance investment
cost_body:
Metal Roofing Cost

Metal roofing in our service area typically runs between $12,000 and $40,000, depending on roof size, profile (standing seam vs. metal shingle), and material (steel, aluminum, or copper). The upfront cost is higher than asphalt, but the system often outlasts two or three asphalt replacements.
benefits_body:
Benefits of Metal Roofing

  • 40-70 year lifespan with proper installation
  • Withstands sustained winds over 140 mph
  • Reflects solar heat — lower cooling costs
  • Class A fire rating
  • Resists salt-air corrosion when properly specified
  • May qualify for insurance discounts in coastal zones
faq_body:
How long does a metal roof last?

A properly installed metal system lasts 40 to 70 years, depending on material. Standing seam steel and aluminum systems are the most common in coastal applications.

Will my home be louder in the rain?

No. Modern metal roofs are installed over solid decking and underlayment, making them no louder than asphalt shingles.

Can metal roofing be installed over my existing roof?

In some cases, yes — but for coastal Carolina homes we recommend a complete tear-off to inspect decking and ensure proper underlayment. We'll give you a clear recommendation during the inspection.

name: Insurance Claim
short_description: Free claim assistance from inspection through final payment — we work with your adjuster directly.
meta_description: Roof insurance claim assistance from Linta Roofing. We meet your adjuster, document the damage, handle supplements, and ensure the scope of work matches the loss. No upfront cost.
hero_heading:
Insurance Claim Assistance

Most homeowners only file a roof insurance claim once or twice in a lifetime — your adjuster files them every day. Linta Roofing levels the playing field. We document the damage, meet your adjuster on-site, handle supplements, and ensure the scope of work matches the actual loss.

Get Free Claim ReviewContact Us
signs_body:
When to Get Claim Help

  • You've experienced wind, hail, or hurricane damage
  • Your initial claim was denied or partially approved
  • The settlement amount seems too low for the damage
  • You need a supplement for items the adjuster missed
  • You're unsure whether to file a claim at all
  • Your adjuster's scope doesn't match what your contractor sees
cost_body:
Cost of Claim Assistance

Linta Roofing's claim assistance is free when paired with our restoration work. We're paid by your insurance carrier, not by you — your only out-of-pocket cost is your homeowner's deductible. We'll explain every line item before you sign anything.
benefits_body:
Why Use a Contractor for Your Claim

  • Adjuster meetings handled by an experienced roofer, not a homeowner
  • Damage documented with photos, drone imagery, and detailed reports
  • Supplements filed for items missed in the original scope
  • Direct billing to your carrier when permitted
  • Familiar with carrier-specific policies and South Carolina / North Carolina building codes
faq_body:
Should I file a claim before or after I call you?

Call us first. We'll inspect your roof for free and tell you whether the damage rises to the level of a claim. A denied claim can affect future premiums.

What does a public adjuster do that you don't?

Public adjusters charge a percentage of the settlement. Our claim assistance is free with the restoration work, and as licensed roofers we have an advantage during the on-site inspection.

Can you help with a claim that was already denied?

Yes — we frequently re-open and reverse denied claims through proper documentation and supplement filings.
